sast_review.reason: The code uses `os.path.splitext(file_item.filename)[1]` to extract the extension from the user-provided filename. If the filename contains path separators (e.g., `../../evil.txt`), the extension extraction may still include dangerous characters (e.g., `.txt` after a slash), and the resulting `unique_filename` can contain path traversal sequences when joined with UPLOAD_DIR. No sanitization or validation is applied to the extension before writing the file.

raw.summary: The code has a path injection vulnerability (CWE-22) in the file upload handler. The filename extension from the user-supplied filename is concatenated with a UUID without sanitizing path separators or special characters, allowing an attacker to control part of the stored file path. This can lead to writing files outside the intended uploads directory.
